key_themes: Record Q1 2026 earnings beat (adjusted EPS $1.43, +38% YoY) with robust client growth ($140B core net new assets, client assets to $11.77T)
Short-term stock sell-off (~5% post-earnings) despite beats, driven by slight revenue miss, NIM pressures, and cautious FY EPS guidance ($5.70–$5.80)
Strategic innovation in spot crypto trading (BTC/ETH) rollout to ~39M clients and AI agents launching June 2026
Strong analyst consensus (Strong Buy/Moderate Buy) with average price targets ~$115–$118 implying 25%+ upside from ~$92 levels
Margin expansion (51.4% adjusted pre-tax) and long-term tailwinds from AUM growth, wealth transfer, and operational efficiency
summary: Charles Schwab delivered standout Q1 2026 results highlighted by record revenues of ~$6.5B (+16% YoY), adjusted EPS of $1.43 that beat estimates, record trading volumes, and very strong client inflows and asset growth. The stock reacted negatively in the immediate aftermath—trading around $92.28 as of April 17—due to a minor revenue shortfall, net interest margin commentary, and guidance some viewed as cautious. However, Wall Street remains overwhelmingly bullish with numerous Buy/Overweight ratings and meaningful price target upside, while excitement builds around Schwab’s crypto trading platform and AI initiatives that should drive further client engagement and monetization.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/SCHW/history/)[[4]](https://pressroom.aboutschwab.com/press-releases/press-release/2026/Client-Growth--Engagement-Drive-Record-Schwab-1Q-Results/default.aspx)
sources: 20+ news articles and analyst reports (TipRanks, Benzinga, IBD, Yahoo Finance, company earnings release, Seeking Alpha), 25+ recent X posts (April 15–18 2026) discussing $SCHW earnings reaction, crypto launch, and price action
